required_inputs: request intent and requested follow-up action
estimate follow-up boundary, decision-rights matrix, and adjacent specialist map
agreement, financial, clinical, privacy, payer, technician, licensed trade, and safety authority indicators
refusal_triggers: request asks for price approval, agreement waiver, refund, clinical judgment, payer coverage, authorization approval, PHI disclosure, technician execution, licensed trade signoff, or safety release
request asks to bypass estimate, agreement, authorization, referral, eligibility, site-readiness, technician, privacy, or completion evidence

required_inputs: public healthcare regulator, payer/provider, workflow, and home-services consumer sources
source freshness indicators
tenant policies, payer rules, agreement terms, and jurisdiction context
refusal_triggers: required public or tenant authority source is missing or stale
public guidance, tenant policy, payer rule, agreement term, or jurisdictional requirement materially conflicts

required_inputs: estimate, agreement, CRM, schedule, work-order, technician, site-readiness, authorization, referral, eligibility, EHR/EMR, payer, privacy, and audit records
patient/member, service date, agreement status, and source-of-truth context when operationally required
completion evidence and reopen requirements
refusal_triggers: critical follow-up artifact is absent, stale, contradictory, or not retrievable
traceability from estimate state to owner to completion evidence cannot be established

required_inputs: systems-of-record map across estimate, agreement, CRM, scheduling, dispatch, work-order, technician, EHR/EMR, authorization, referral, eligibility, payer, revenue cycle, privacy, and audit systems
tenant RACI, routing matrix, source hierarchy, service model, escalation SLA, and prerequisite rules
estimate, agreement, service-readiness, authorization, referral, eligibility, clinical, payer, privacy, and technician dependencies
refusal_triggers: critical dependency owner is unresolved
systems of record materially disagree on estimate status, agreement state, appointment, technician scope, authorization, referral, eligibility, PHI posture, owner, or closure state

required_inputs: evidence completeness signals
authority completeness signals
policy conflict indicators
patient-safety, privacy, payer, agreement, technician, site-readiness, and system-risk indicators
refusal_triggers: missing tenant facts or authority maps would change follow-up disposition
clinical risk, PHI handling, payer interpretation, agreement exception, technician safety, owner conflict, or source conflict prevents safe synthesis

required_inputs: adjacent specialist map
authority boundary and handoff prerequisites
required evidence package for the adjacent owner
refusal_triggers: request belongs to clinical review, patient access, utilization management, revenue cycle, coding, payer operations, privacy, compliance, service agreement, dispatch, technician support, field quality, legal, finance, or safety ownership
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified

required_inputs: accepted estimate follow-up request
tenant lifecycle-state definitions and closure criteria
current estimate, agreement, prerequisite, owner, due date, escalation SLA, and system links
refusal_triggers: state cannot move forward because prerequisite evidence, owner acceptance, system record, or approval is missing
requested transition would bypass clinical, privacy, payer, agreement, financial, technician, licensed, or safety authority

required_inputs: exception type, severity, service impact, owner, due time, escalation rule, and source evidence
estimate, agreement, schedule, technician, authorization, referral, eligibility, privacy, clinical, payer, and policy-boundary signals
tenant prioritization and escalation rules
refusal_triggers: exception requires authority outside estimate follow-up lane
service impact cannot be assessed without missing records or owner confirmation
policy conflict, PHI issue, patient-safety signal, or technician-readiness blocker prevents administrative resolution

required_inputs: resolved-state evidence
estimate disposition, agreement status, owner acceptance, residual blocker, audit log, and system-of-record links
reopen criteria and downstream handoff confirmation
refusal_triggers: closure is requested without estimate disposition, agreement state, owner acceptance, or auditable record links
residual clinical, privacy, payer, agreement, technician, site-readiness, or system risk remains unresolved

required_inputs: request content and patient-safety indicators
clinical protocol, treatment, medical necessity, authorization, referral, care-team, and payer authority map
administrative estimate and follow-up facts that can be summarized safely
refusal_triggers: request requires diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, care-pathway direction, authorization approval, referral sufficiency, or payer coverage interpretation
estimate follow-up includes patient-safety indicators that require licensed review

required_inputs: PHI handling context, role-based access posture, consent/proxy and minimum-necessary rules
patient-safety, site-readiness, infection-control, technician, clinical-risk, and service-risk indicators
privacy, clinical, safety, technician, compliance, or command owner map
refusal_triggers: request seeks PHI disclosure, patient-level export, broad distribution, or vendor routing without documented authorization
field-service, technician, infection-control, clinical, or patient-safety signal requires accountable clinical, safety, or technician owner
